records.ety: [F. secret (cf. Sp. & Pg. secreto, It. secreto, segreto), fr. L. secretus, p. p. of secernere to put apart, to separate. See Certain, and cf. Secrete, Secern.]

defs: Hidden; concealed; as, secret treasure; secret plans; a secret vow.
quotes: The secret things belong unto the Lord our God; but those things which are revealed belong unto us.

defs: Withdrawn from general intercourse or notice; in retirement or secrecy; secluded.
quotes: There, secret in her sapphire cell, He with the Nais wont to dwell.

defs: Faithful to a secret; not inclined to divulge or betray confidence; secretive.
quotes: Secret Romans, that have spoke the word, And will not palter.
They suppose two other divine hypostases superior thereunto, which were perfectly secret from matter.

records.ety: [F. secret (cf. Pr. secret, Sp. & Pg. secreto, It. secreto, segreto), from L. secretum. See Secret, a.]

defs: Something studiously concealed; a thing kept from general knowledge; what is not revealed, or not to be revealed.
quotes: To tell our own secrets is often folly; to communicate those of others is treachery.

defs: A thing not discovered; what is unknown or unexplained; a mystery.
quotes: All secrets of the deep, all nature's works.

defs: The parts which modesty and propriety require to be concealed; the genital organs.
quotes: Bread eaten in secret is pleasant.
